mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns do not exceed 55% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 6.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals severe earnings headwinds, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 6.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer and investment demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns with a win rate above 50% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp month-over-month contractions in industrial production reflect immediate supply-side shocks that trigger short-term equity sell-offs, yielding negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero during periods of industrial production contraction.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of near-zero policy rates and rising unemployment indicates liquidity traps where monetary easing fails to stimulate growth, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity returns turn positive when policy rates fall below 1.0 percent alongside rising unemployment, contradicting the liquidity trap mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales crossing below 2 percent signals a severe breakdown in consumer spending power, forecasting negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns exceed 55% positive outcomes when retail sales drop more than 2 percent month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating nonfarm payroll losses reflect deteriorating corporate revenue expectations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no significant negative bias during months with payroll declines exceeding 200,000.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index values erode real corporate profits and force tighter financial conditions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in over 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL increases month-over-month.
